title: malpighia harrisii subsp. harrisii
intro: Malpighia harrisii subsp. harrisii, also known as the Jamaican fruit bush, is a shrub native to the Caribbean region, particularly in Jamaica, and is a subspecies of the Malpighia harrisii plant. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat wounds, fever, and other ailments due to its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of the Jamaican fruit bush include its potential to reduce blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and possess antioxidant properties. The plant is typically prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
